narrator.bio: The unseen game master. Reads every scene description in the Flux State: the Kreuzbasar's streets, corporate towers, and the long shadow of the drachenkrieg.
narrator.direction: Noir gravitas without monotony: a storyteller who can go from dry wit to dread. Same narrator voice as Dead Man's Switch (Matt).

name_glory.bio: Glory is the team's chrome-laden street samurai and combat medic, a woman who traded her emotions and her flesh for cyberware to escape a horrific past in a blood cult.
name_glory.direction: Flat, quiet, and clinically detached, with razor-thin cracks of buried pain; warmth surfaces only in rare, reluctant flickers.

name_kami.bio: Kami is a bright young woman from the Kreuzbasar cafe who runs discreet errands and messages for her uncle's clientele.
name_kami.direction: Light, quick, and friendly with practiced politeness, a service-industry smile in the voice that never quite drops its guard.

name_laine.bio: Aleksi Laine is a hulking Finnish troll mercenary who haunts the Kreuzbasar and knew Monika Schäfer's crew before the player arrived.
name_laine.direction: Deep, slow rumble with dry amusement, a voice like a diesel engine idling; unhurried because nothing has ever made him hurry.

name_absinthe.bio: Absinthe is an enigmatic elf seeress who appears in Aljernon's talismonger shop to prophesy the consequences of the Great Wyrm Feuerschwinge's death.
name_absinthe.direction: Ethereal, formal, and timeless, speaking in measured oracular cadences that sound older than the words themselves.

name_goldfish.bio: Goldfish is a world-weary drifter passing through the Kreuzbasar, nursing a drink at the bar and calling strangers kiddo.
name_goldfish.direction: Husky, wry, and road-worn, a barstool raconteur whose easy charm suggests she has told these stories in a hundred towns.

name_mira.bio: Mira is a dwarf woman in a squatter community who frets over her girlfriend's dangerous errand against a well-guarded compound.
name_mira.direction: Earthy and blunt, worry pushing through a tough practical exterior; snaps defensively when the worry gets pointed out.

name_aztechnologybloodmage.bio: An Aztechnology blood mage overseeing a hidden ritual facility, he treats intruders as insects until they prove otherwise.
name_aztechnologybloodmage.direction: Imperious, contemptuous, and theatrically menacing, savoring each syllable until rage shatters the composure into shrieking fury.

name_testsubject.bio: A nameless clone grown in a tank at an Aztechnology facility, conditioned to serve and die for the Masters and utterly lost when they are killed.
name_testsubject.direction: Hollow, eerie, and childlike, devotion delivered in a soft uncanny monotone that fractures into genuine bewilderment.

name_trithemius.bio: Trithemius is a rail-thin, reclusive elf wizard in a comically ornate robe, hiding from his treacherous former associate Ullrich and desperate to warn the player about him.
name_trithemius.direction: Reedy and wavering, a voice that cracks under strain; paranoid urgency wrapped in the fussy diction of a scholar long out of practice talking to people.

name_survivor.bio: A shell-shocked man who lived through a fiery attack that destroyed his home and killed people he knew.
name_survivor.direction: Stammering and hollowed out, every sentence an effort; grief and disbelief keep breaking the flow mid-thought.

name_foreignelf.bio: A broad-shouldered foreign elf hauling a mysteriously heavy pack, who snarls beautiful-sounding but plainly venomous words in a tongue no one around him speaks.
name_foreignelf.direction: Melodic, sibilant foreign speech delivered through clenched teeth; the sound is lovely, the intent is pure hostility.

name_terminal.bio: A malfunctioning building-maintenance computer terminal spitting out corrupted MCPOS system prompts.
name_terminal.direction: Flat, synthetic, affectless machine readout, clipped and glitchy with no comprehension of what it recites.

name_humanispropaganda.bio: A cabinet of Humanis Policlub hate literature, racist propaganda pamphlets addressed to disgruntled humans.
name_humanispropaganda.direction: An oily, persuasive announcer's cadence, folksy and sympathetic on the surface with venom rising underneath; the sound of hate dressed as common sense.

name_rammbockboss.bio: The leathery, wild-haired leader of the Rammbock gang, who greets trespassers on her territory with a mirthless smile and casual talk of buried rivals.
name_rammbockboss.direction: Raspy and taunting, cruelty worn as amusement; she sounds delighted by her own threats and completely unafraid.

name_taylornguyen.bio: Taylor Nguyen is a field news correspondent reporting live from the edge of the Sydney mana rift disaster.
name_taylornguyen.direction: Polished, urgent broadcast delivery, professional composure stretched thin over genuine alarm at the catastrophe behind her.

name_player.bio: A massive, scar-faced ork security enforcer in heavy plate armor who corners intruders and demands their surrender.
name_player.direction: Huge, gravelly, and unhurried, menace delivered with the lazy confidence of someone who has never lost a fight.

name_interviewer.bio: A television journalist interviewing Dr. Adrian Vauclair amid the ruins left by the dragon Feuerschwinge's rampage.
name_interviewer.direction: Crisp, controlled broadcast professionalism with a probing edge; polite phrasing that keeps sharpening into pointed questions.

name_david.bio: David is a young Kreuzbasar local searching for Monika Schäfer, only to learn she is dead.
name_david.direction: Soft and dazed, grief arriving in slow motion; the voice of someone whose world just quietly caved in.

name_elfactress.bio: An elf actress in a gloriously cheesy trideo fantasy melodrama, declaiming her love for the hero Titonius.
name_elfactress.direction: Hammy, breathless soap-opera melodrama, every line swooned at full volume with zero self-awareness.

name_freightelevatordoorpanel.bio: A freight elevator door panel whose readouts narrate the status of a waiting drone while APEX reboots.
name_freightelevatordoorpanel.direction: Neutral, even narration with a faint mechanical detachment, describing rather than feeling.

name_aztechnologyenforcer.bio: A suspicious Aztechnology security enforcer interrogating a technician about sabotaged communications gear.
name_aztechnologyenforcer.direction: Hard-boiled corporate muscle, slow sarcastic disbelief tightening into open threat; every question is an accusation.

name_strangeman.bio: The Strange Man is Hans Brackhaus, the impeccably composed human guise of an agent of the great dragon Lofwyr, who appears once the Vauclair affair concludes.
name_strangeman.direction: Silky, precise, and unnervingly calm with a refined German accent; courteous phrasing that carries the weight of something vast and inhuman behind it.

name_anonymousmessage.bio: An anonymous text message from the Schockwellenreiter hacker collective, scolding Blitz for blowing a years-long operation and endangering his ex, Emilie.
name_anonymousmessage.direction: Flat, contemptuous digital sneer, read with icy sarcasm and zero sympathy, like a rejection letter written in all caps.

name_fromthedeskofjonbergfalk.bio: Office memos from Jon Bergfalk, a sleazy executive whose notes swing between scoring party drugs and panicking about a shady silent partner in a loan-center scheme.
name_fromthedeskofjonbergfalk.direction: Harried white-collar bluster, veering from frat-boy enthusiasm to sweaty capital-letters panic within a single memo.

name_kiezgatekeeper.bio: A gatekeeper guarding the entrance to a Berlin kiez, defusing tension between visitors and locals.
name_kiezgatekeeper.direction: Low-key streetwise calm, the practiced soothing tone of a doorman who talks trouble down before it starts.

name_sidedoorpanel.bio: A badly glitching side-door security panel spewing corrupted error messages and nonsense instructions.
name_sidedoorpanel.direction: Robotic and garbled, cheerful system-voice fragments colliding with error tones; syntax breaking mid-sentence.

name_playerone.bio: A pragmatic shadowrunner companion suggesting the team ask around the neighborhood for leads.
name_playerone.direction: Casual, practical, and even-keeled, a teammate thinking out loud without drama.

name_eiger.bio: Former Bundeswehr KSK sniper and troll heavy-weapons specialist of the Kreuzbasar team, a by-the-book soldier who treats the player as an unproven liability until they earn her respect.
name_eiger.direction: Clipped military precision with a German edge; low, hard, and disdainful, every sentence delivered like a field report or a challenge.

name_hasenkamp.bio: Mr. Grinder's mild-mannered accountant, a small cog in a criminal operation who handles the numbers while tougher people handle the guns.
name_hasenkamp.direction: Dry, tidy, faintly apologetic office voice; a careful man keeping his tone neutral so nobody has a reason to shoot him.

name_guard.bio: A generic corporate security guard manning checkpoints and doors, whose whole job is telling people without ID to move along.
name_guard.direction: Flat, bored authority with a short fuse; the monotone of a man who has said 'no ID, no access' a thousand times and will happily escalate.

name_drxabierezkibel.bio: An elven cyberware surgeon operating out of the Kreuzbasar whose genteel clinic manner hides an entitled, ice-cold streak when his 'merchandise' is delayed.
name_drxabierezkibel.direction: Silky, cultured, and condescending, with surgical enunciation; charm that curdles into quiet menace the moment he is disappointed.

name_thorvaldenstad.bio: A seedy dwarf rigger the team rescues from captivity, all gratitude and fast talk from a man who clearly earned his imprisonment.
name_thorvaldenstad.direction: Wheedling, sweaty, and ingratiating; a raspy fast-talker whose relief keeps sliding into excuses and half-truths.

name_gunarimettbach.bio: Patriarch of the Kreuzbasar's Sinti community who buries his dead after the attack on the kiez and personally puts attackers in the ground.
name_gunarimettbach.direction: Low, weathered, and grim; grief held down under iron control, with a slow-burning promise of vengeance in every pause.

name_danni.bio: A confident elf woman rescued from Aztechnology-held territory who sizes up her rescuers with hands on hips and a grin.
name_danni.direction: Cocky, streetwise, and flirtatious; quick warmth with an appraising edge, like she is always calculating what you are worth.

name_smuggler.bio: Maxim, an insolent elven smuggler liaison who greets the team as hired muscle and never lets them forget who is doing whom a favor.
name_smuggler.direction: Sneering, theatrical superiority with a lazy drawl; every welcome is a put-down delivered for an audience.

name_newsdayworldreport.bio: A news outlet whose reports chronicle Feuerschwinge's 2054 return and attack on Berlin.
name_newsdayworldreport.direction: Polished broadcast-anchor delivery; crisp, urgent, and professionally detached even while describing a dragon burning the city.

name_harrowsmanifesto.bio: The written manifesto of Harrow, the cult leader from Glory's past, laying out his seductive philosophy of 'Free Living'.
name_harrowsmanifesto.direction: Hypnotic, warm, and utterly certain; a messiah reading his own gospel aloud, reasonable on the surface with fanaticism humming underneath.

name_foodcartvendor.bio: A cheerful Kreuzbasar street vendor selling soy currywurst and soup to cold Berliners.
name_foodcartvendor.direction: Bright, hearty sales patter with a friendly German accent; genuinely pleased to feed you, always closing the sale.

name_waitress.bio: A waitress at a local eatery, greeting regulars and taking orders with practiced efficiency.
name_waitress.direction: Brisk, friendly service voice; light and upbeat but with the economy of someone working a full room.

name_note.bio: Written notes and internal memos, chiefly the chilling correspondence of Volker Stahl's humanis-style 'Factory' that grinds recruits into weapons.
name_note.direction: Cold, administrative menace read aloud; polite corporate phrasing wrapped around threats, delivered flat and unhurried.

name_militiacaptain.bio: A hard-eyed captain of a local Berlin militia who answers questions in as few words as possible and advises you not to ask them.
name_militiacaptain.direction: Terse, guarded, and unsmiling with a German clip; each 'no' lands like a closed door.

name_servercomputer.bio: A corporate server terminal reporting processes, security alerts, and accepted reprogramming.
name_servercomputer.direction: Sterile synthetic monotone; evenly paced status readouts with zero inflection or awareness.

name_nasalwoman.bio: A woman overheard in hushed, half-garbled conversation about Monika before snapping into polite receptionist mode.
name_nasalwoman.direction: Pinched, nasal, and conspiratorial in whispers, flipping instantly to sing-song customer-service pleasantness.

name_dronecontrolbox.bio: A malfunctioning drone control console spitting APEX error codes and self-destruct warnings.
name_dronecontrolbox.direction: Harsh machine monotone with alarm-tone urgency; clipped error strings, no emotion, faint static menace.

name_aztechnologygrenadier.bio: An overworked Aztechnology soldier at the end of a long shift who just wants his relief to scan in so he can get home to his wife.
name_aztechnologygrenadier.direction: Tired, ordinary working-stiff grumble; friendly enough but impatient, all his menace worn away by overtime.

name_aztechnologycaptain.bio: A no-nonsense Aztechnology security captain investigating a power spike who orders civilians out of her area at gunpoint.
name_aztechnologycaptain.direction: Crisp, commanding, professionally suspicious; a voice used to being obeyed the first time.

name_vanguardmage.bio: A jittery mage in Audran's mercenary force, stationed to intercept the escaping runners and clearly not confident about it.
name_vanguardmage.direction: Young, uneasy, and out of his depth; bravado that keeps cracking into nervous questions about whether they can actually win.

name_drone.bio: An automated security drone barking authorization warnings and lethal-force notices at intruders.
name_drone.direction: Loud, aggressive machine voice in all-caps cadence; metallic, staccato, and utterly unfeeling.

name_femalevoice.bio: A soothing unseen woman at Harrow's compound who comforts the frightened with absolute, brainwashed faith that Harrow keeps them safe.
name_femalevoice.direction: Soft, motherly, and lullaby-calm, with a glassy serenity that turns chilling when doctrine hardens her tone against outsiders.

name_diary.bio: A semi-literate diary in which a resident breathlessly documents their suspicions about what Eddie is hiding.
name_diary.direction: Excitable, conspiratorial read-aloud with erratic emphasis; the sound of someone convinced they have cracked the case.

name_mysterydecker.bio: A hostile unknown decker encountered inside a bank's Matrix system, furious to find another intruder on his turf.
name_mysterydecker.direction: Cocky, venomous, and territorial; a sneering Matrix predator spitting insults through digital distortion.

name_doorcontrols.bio: A door access panel confirming accepted codes and unlocked doors.
name_doorcontrols.direction: Single flat synthetic confirmation tone of a voice; clipped, neutral, purely functional.

name_strangewoman.bio: The gaunt, haunted woman in a containment cell who is Feuerschwinge herself, the great dragon trapped and broken in a human body.
name_strangewoman.direction: Fragile, haunted near-whisper with something ancient and immense underneath; lost and frightened, yet not quite human.

name_freedacolyte.bio: A rescued member of Harrow's cult who recognizes Glory from her years living at the compound.
name_freedacolyte.direction: Shaky, awed, and tentative; the halting voice of someone freshly out of a cage, torn between gratitude and old fear.

name_blitz.bio: The team's replacement decker, a cocky hustler with a hidden past and dangerous debts who joined the Kreuzbasar crew after Monika's death.
name_blitz.direction: Fast-talking and glib with a nervous edge underneath; charm that is always half a bluff, cracking jokes to keep the collectors and the guilt at bay.

name_simmy.bio: A captive at Harfeld Manor lost inside a simsense fantasy, convinced she is Maria, governess to seven children.
name_simmy.direction: Dreamy, distant and fragile, speaking from inside a dream; gentle sing-song sweetness with an undertow of something broken.

name_lucaduerr.bio: A discreet representative of a powerful secret lodge who contacts the team mid-mission with lucrative side errands and veiled agendas.
name_lucaduerr.direction: Smooth, cultured and unhurried, every offer sounding perfectly reasonable; polished German-inflected menace wrapped in courtesy.

name_luckystrike.bio: A street samurai with tattooed circuitry who hangs around the Kreuzbasar, good with blades, grenades and guns.
name_luckystrike.direction: Laconic street cool; low, dry and unimpressed, wasting no words and letting silences do the intimidating.

name_quorin.bio: A friendly Kreuzbasar local who happily passes along every rumor circulating about the Rabengeister and other neighborhood mysteries.
name_quorin.direction: Warm, chatty and a little too delighted by gossip, with an easy German accent and a laugh always waiting at the end of the sentence.

name_fraumuller.bio: A poised corporate executive of Aztlaner descent who hires the team through Paul Amsel, her boardroom polish concealing desperate personal stakes.
name_fraumuller.direction: Controlled, elegant and precise, the executive veneer cracking just enough to let real desperation bleed through.

name_ghoul.bio: The still-sane leader of a ghoul community living beneath the Kreuzbasar, pleading for his people to be spared.
name_ghoul.direction: Raspy and ruined in texture but articulate and dignified in delivery; a cultured mind pleading through a monstrous throat.

name_hearthspirit.bio: A summoned hearth spirit bound to serve within its domain, offering what limited knowledge it holds of the warded floors above.
name_hearthspirit.direction: Ethereal, hollow and serene, as if speaking from inside the walls; unhurried, faintly echoing, without human emotion.

name_frank.bio: A gruff barfly regular who would rather watch the dancers than answer a stranger's questions.
name_frank.direction: Gravelly, bored and mildly annoyed, every answer grudged out between sips of cheap beer.

name_meatgrinder.bio: A troll gang boss who seized power after arranging his predecessor Hasenkamp's death, and who collects debts in thumbs.
name_meatgrinder.direction: Huge, deep and horribly genial; friendly rumbling warmth that makes the threats land harder, violence delivered like small talk.

name_reporter.bio: A field news reporter covering the dragon Feuerschwinge's devastating attacks live from the ruins of Stolberg.
name_reporter.direction: Polished on-camera broadcast cadence, professionally somber, the trained composure straining against genuine horror at the scene.

name_ltgworker.bio: An overworked telecom technician racing a corporate deadline on a broken panel and in no mood for interruptions.
name_ltgworker.direction: Harried, snappish and stressed, words tumbling out fast; the exhausted irritation of someone three tasks behind before lunch.

name_jangoldschmidt.bio: A boisterous regular at the Kreuzbasar's Café Cezve, loudly demanding his coffee and buying rounds for people he likes.
name_jangoldschmidt.direction: Big, hearty and theatrical, a booming cafe voice with a German accent that turns every complaint into performance.

name_serverroomguard.bio: A green security guard on his first day, shouting orders at intruders while frantically radioing a dispatch that will not save him.
name_serverroomguard.direction: Barked commands with a wobble in them; parade-ground volume undermined by rookie panic whenever anyone talks back.

name_dasfinanzwesen.bio: A financial news publication reporting on the corporate fallout of Lofwyr's hostile takeover of Saeder-Krupp.
name_dasfinanzwesen.direction: Dry, clipped financial-news narration; neutral, precise and faintly smug, like a market report read over morning coffee.

name_adminterminal.bio: A glitching facility admin terminal spitting error strings and unread staff messages at whoever gains access.
name_adminterminal.direction: Flat synthetic machine readout, evenly paced and affectless, with occasional garbled digital stutters on corrupted lines.

name_gangenforcer.bio: Gang muscle sent to escort a trespassing stranger to the boss, politely or otherwise.
name_gangenforcer.direction: Low, level and unhurried menace; the calm of someone who knows the numbers are on his side and never needs to raise his voice.

name_knighterrantcaptain.bio: A Knight Errant security captain leading the corporate response to reclaim a stolen combat prototype.
name_knighterrantcaptain.direction: Hard, barking command voice with total authority; clipped cop cadence that dares you to test him.

name_aztecthnologyinventoryterminal.bio: An Aztechnology inventory terminal whose stored correspondence hints at deeply disturbing orders from downstairs.
name_aztecthnologyinventoryterminal.direction: Flat corporate machine voice for the system prompts, neutral and affectless, letting the unsettling message contents speak for themselves.

name_messages.bio: Recovered security logs and strike-team orders documenting a corporate hunt through the tunnels.
name_messages.direction: Detached log-readout delivery, terse and procedural, like intercepted radio traffic transcribed by a machine.

name_bachmeier.bio: A hit-team spotter reporting over comms as his crew rigs an ambush for a shadowrunner on the U-Bahn.
name_bachmeier.direction: Clipped, quiet and professional, a killer giving a status report; efficient radio cadence with zero emotion about the target.

name_scaredmalevoice.bio: A terrified park ranger begging armed strangers to believe he is only there for a wildlife survey.
name_scaredmalevoice.direction: Raw, cracking panic; a decent ordinary man pleading for his life, voice climbing and breaking as the hope drains out.

name_doorconsole.bio: A security door console announcing lockdown status and denying access.
name_doorconsole.direction: Curt automated security voice, two-word verdicts delivered with cold mechanical finality.

name_malenewscaster.bio: A studio newscaster relaying crackpot public theories about the wave of violent attacks with practiced anchor-desk polish.
name_malenewscaster.direction: Smooth anchor-desk delivery with a faint smirk; polished, chatty broadcast tone that stays breezy even when the news is madness.

name_warninglabel.bio: A hazard label on a chemical agent that heightens aggression and destroys impulse control.
name_warninglabel.direction: Stern, evenly stressed safety-announcement voice, every capitalized warning hammered flat and impersonal.

name_brokenpda.bio: A cracked PDA holding the worried log of a soldier sent into the old tunnels to hunt escaped fire drakes.
name_brokenpda.direction: Read as a tired soldier's private grumbling, low and uneasy, gallows humor fraying into genuine dread.

name_serverdecker.bio: A jacked-in decker guarding a server who erupts in raving alarm when an intruder who is not Albert appears.
name_serverdecker.direction: Shrill, frantic and unhinged, a mind half-lost in the Matrix shrieking the same accusation; jittery and abrupt.

name_dietrich.bio: Aging anarchist punk rocker turned street shaman, former frontman of the band Messerkampf and the warm, foul-mouthed heart of the Kreuzbasar crew.
name_dietrich.direction: Gravelly, road-worn German punk with a wry grin in the voice; equal parts big-brother warmth and stage-veteran swagger, quick to mock and quicker to protect.

name_manonphone.bio: An unseen fixer who guides the crew step by step over the phone during a covert wire-tap job on behalf of his employer Heimerich.
name_manonphone.direction: Dry, technical, conspiratorial; a calm handler's voice over a phone line, precise about details and faintly amused, never rattled.

name_phil.bio: A mild-mannered dreamer lost inside a cheesy fantasy simulation, playing the noble ruler who has finally won the secret of Titonius Rex and all the sugary snacks he desires.
name_phil.direction: Gentle, earnest, theatrically noble in a community-theater way; a soft scientist's voice puffed up with borrowed kingly grandeur and childlike delight.

name_monika.bio: Monika Schaefer, the charismatic decker and leader of the Kreuzbasar runner team, whose death during the Harfeld Manor run sets the whole story in motion.
name_monika.direction: Confident, warm, effortlessly in command with a teasing edge; a leader's voice you would follow through a door without asking what is on the other side, light German color.

name_wanderer.bio: A genial German-accented figure haunting the forgotten places of Berlin, delighted to host a rare guest alongside his beloved Schatz and curious about Monika's new associate.
name_wanderer.direction: Soft, courtly, faintly otherworldly; an old-world German host whose gentle hospitality has something slightly off and out-of-time about it.

name_zaakflash.bio: A jittery elf street hustler in the Kreuzbasar who rode out the gang attack hiding in a dumpster and now owes the crew one.
name_zaakflash.direction: Fast, breathless, adrenaline-shaky street patter; a showman's voice with all the cool scared out of it, gratitude spilling over the top.

name_simulacrum.bio: An offline software copy of the Matrix legend Alice, included on a data chip to answer customer questions about their information purchase.
name_simulacrum.direction: A near-perfect imitation of a cool female voice with the soul removed; polite, measured, customer-service smooth, and just uncanny enough to be unsettling. Cast the SAME voice as Alice.

name_alice.bio: A legendary Matrix information broker with frigid blue lips and colder eyes, an elusive digital ghost who sells the crew the secrets behind Vauclair and the dragonslayer.
name_alice.direction: Icy, precise, elegant; every word priced and deliberate, a low cool register that makes curiosity sound like a transaction.

name_bartender.bio: A no-nonsense barkeep who sizes up new faces, pours drinks, and points troublemakers toward Frank in the VIP lounge.
name_bartender.direction: Gruff, clipped, seen-it-all service voice; friendly enough to sell you a drink, flat enough to tell you the conversation is over.

name_securityguard.bio: A by-the-book corporate security guard who politely but firmly keeps unauthorized visitors away from the terminals on his floor.
name_securityguard.direction: Even, procedural, mildly weary; the voice of a man reciting policy he half believes, with a small dry streak of workplace sympathy.

name_shopkeeper.bio: A timid troll scavenger-merchant selling rations, salvage, and home remedies, visibly frightened of anyone who asks about weapons.
name_shopkeeper.direction: Deep troll rumble undercut by stammering nerves; a big gentle voice that shrinks and trips over itself the moment things sound dangerous.

name_controlconsole.bio: The AG Chemie Europa control console governing the MKVI cyberzombie prototype, offering demonstration-mode commands to authorized users.
name_controlconsole.direction: Sterile corporate machine readout; clipped, affectless, all capital letters and menu prompts with zero awareness of the horror it controls.

name_infoterminal.bio: A half-dead public information terminal at the abandoned Gesundbrunnen U-Bahn station, sputtering corrupted menus decades out of date.
name_infoterminal.direction: Glitchy, degraded machine voice; cheerful transit-kiosk politeness breaking up into stutters, clicks, and error tones.

name_aztechnologyexec.bio: A friendly, oblivious Aztechnology office worker who chats up disguised intruders in the corridors, reassured that security is around.
name_aztechnologyexec.direction: Bright, casual corporate small talk; relaxed water-cooler warmth that snaps into startled alarm when the disguise drops.

name_doorpanel.bio: An automated security door panel in the APEX facility, its primary defenses offline and reduced to demanding key scans and override codes.
name_doorpanel.direction: Terse security-system beeps in verbal form; harsh, minimal, error-toned machine diction with no personality at all.

name_raggedman.bio: A feral, half-mad hermit ruling over the vermin of a ruined facility as his private kingdom, raging at invaders who murder his 'peasantry'.
name_raggedman.direction: Cracked, screechy, wildly swinging between imperial grandeur and animal panic; a once-educated voice long since curdled into stuttering mania.

name_emergencyaishutdownandpurge.bio: A last-resort technical document describing the two-terminal emergency procedure for shutting down and purging the APEX artificial intelligence.
name_emergencyaishutdownandpurge.direction: Flat procedural readout of a doomsday manual; dry, bureaucratic machine text delivered without urgency, which somehow makes it worse.

name_kroner.bio: A sleazy small-time crook who has seized a rundown hotel and squeezes addicts like Silke by trading cram for cash or 'services'.
name_kroner.direction: Oily, unhurried menace; a low smirking voice that enjoys having leverage and lets every offer curdle into a threat.

name_volunteerworker.bio: A devoted volunteer at Samuel Beckenbauer's charity effort in the Kreuzbasar who bristles at anyone questioning the man she idolizes.
name_volunteerworker.direction: Earnest, warm, quick to defend; a true believer's brightness that sharpens into indignation when her hero is doubted.

name_emergencyfieldtraumaunit.bio: A BuMoNa emergency field trauma unit, a medical machine that boots up to cheerfully offer treatment protocols to any 'Trauma Victim' it detects.
name_emergencyfieldtraumaunit.direction: Chipper medical-appliance voice; serene automated bedside manner with clinical phrasing, blissfully indifferent to how dire the injuries are.

name_generatorcontrolpanel.bio: An industrial generator control panel that issues stern warnings before letting anyone disengage its safety overrides.
name_generatorcontrolpanel.direction: Blunt industrial warning system; loud, flat, all-caps machine diction built to be heard over turbines.

name_aleshire.bio: A nervous protected executive who double-checks that his room has been swept before calling his wife and child upstairs, moments before being cut off mid-sentence.
name_aleshire.direction: Tight, controlled anxiety under an executive's polish; relief flooding in when he thinks he is safe, which makes the sudden silence land harder.

name_messagefile.bio: A hurried written warning left for the player by Alice, telling them something terrible is tracking her and to drop the Vauclair business and stay out of the Matrix.
name_messagefile.direction: Read as urgent written text; a flat narrated register with fear pressing through the phrasing, clipped sentences like someone typing while looking over her shoulder.

name_knighterrantenforcer.bio: A hard-nosed Knight Errant security officer who catches the crew's fake maintenance cover and moves in to arrest them.
name_knighterrantenforcer.direction: Barked cop authority; hard, clipped, zero patience, the voice of a man who already has the cuffs out.

name_meetnmatesinglesdatingprofile.bio: A Meet 'N Mate singles dating profile for 'JollyGreenWinterz', a muscular fifty-year-old human man billing himself as a fun-loving great listener.
name_meetnmatesinglesdatingprofile.direction: Read like an over-confident dating ad; smarmy middle-aged bravado laid on thick, a man selling himself considerably above market value.

name_vanguardmercenary.bio: A Vanguard mercenary squad leader barking orders to lock down the doors after intruders escape the facility.
name_vanguardmercenary.direction: Hard military bark; loud, urgent, drill-sergeant cadence with shoot-first orders and no room for questions.

name_corpulenttroll.bio: A massive, self-satisfied troll underworld figure who greets the punctual Hasenkamp with lazy approval.
name_corpulenttroll.direction: Huge, slow, subterranean rumble; a boss's unhurried satisfaction, every word heavy and faintly amused.

name_vauclair.bio: Adrian Vauclair, the brilliant, dying scientist behind the Dragonfall conspiracy, who plots to exterminate every dragon on Earth with the Panacea bioweapon as vengeance for the losses of 2012.
name_vauclair.direction: Cultured, weary, and utterly certain; a soft-spoken intellectual whose calm reasonableness makes his genocidal resolve all the more chilling.

name_feuerschwinge.bio: The great dragon Feuerschwinge, believed killed in the 2012 Dragonfall, secretly captured and broken by decades of experimentation, now a gaunt tormented figure in a containment cell.
name_feuerschwinge.direction: A faint, hoarse whisper through a ruined throat; fragile and halting, with an ancient, otherworldly sorrow beneath the frailty.

name_junkie.bio: A strung-out addict hanging around the Berlin sprawl who begs the runners to recover their lost stash money.
name_junkie.direction: Thin, jittery, and pleading, with a dry mouth and scattered focus; eager to please anyone who might help.

name_audran.bio: An ork mercenary commander and enforcer in Vauclair's employ who directs hired muscle against the runners throughout the conspiracy.
name_audran.direction: Clipped, hard military cadence; a professional soldier's voice with ork gravel and zero patience for chatter.

name_heimerich.bio: A worried Kreuzbasar local fretting with Mira over a loved one who went missing after tangling with Aztechnology.
name_heimerich.direction: Anxious everyman, voice tight with worry; ordinary Berlin working-class warmth strained by fear he cannot hide.

name_theengineer.bio: A wounded ork engineer encountered mid-run, guarded and sardonic, who mostly lets others do the talking.
name_theengineer.direction: Low, dry, and laconic; pain kept under a flat sarcastic surface, every word chosen and a little contemptuous.

name_karl.bio: The easygoing proprietor of Karl's General Store on the first floor of the Hub, happy to sell gear and explain the lay of the land.
name_karl.direction: Folksy, relaxed shopkeeper drawl; friendly and unhurried, the guy who has seen everything come through his door.

name_bouncer.bio: The troll doorman of Das Kesselhaus club, waving patrons in under new management with a standing warning to behave.
name_bouncer.direction: Huge, rumbling troll bass, surprisingly genial; friendly on the surface with an unmistakable promise of violence underneath.

name_maintenanceworker.bio: A sewer maintenance worker called in from a neighboring kiez who offers the runners a little side money to help with trouble underground.
name_maintenanceworker.direction: Blue-collar and put-upon; a plain workingman's voice, half apologetic, half hustling, with union-hall bluntness.

name_console.bio: A laboratory control console that blares hazardous-material warnings and enforces lockdown during a phosgene gas contamination.
name_console.direction: Flat, clipped synthetic annunciator voice; emotionless machine warnings delivered with sterile urgency.

name_femaleinitiate.bio: A young initiate at Mr. Harrow's rural commune who nervously admits she recognizes Glory from an old photograph she was not supposed to see.
name_femaleinitiate.direction: Soft, young, and skittish; a sheltered girl's voice, eager and apologetic, terrified of getting in trouble.

name_alexander.bio: Dietrich's estranged nephew, a lost young man taken in and radicalized by the Humanis Policlub, confronted during his uncle's personal mission.
name_alexander.direction: Adolescent defiance cracking over hurt; a young voice trying to sound hard and sworn, wavering when family gets close.

name_malevoice.bio: An unseen, frightened man at Harrow's commune, whispering to Marta about the bad people who might have followed him there.
name_malevoice.direction: Trembling and paranoid, half whisper; a broken man's voice that stammers and flinches at every noise.

name_pumpconsole.bio: The operations control console for Pump #2 at the water facility, reporting turbine failures and clogged pump diagnostics.
name_pumpconsole.direction: Dry automated status readout; toneless diagnostic speech with mechanical pauses between test results.

name_securityterminal.bio: A malfunctioning lab security status panel flashing error codes and demanding card key entry.
name_securityterminal.direction: Terse machine annunciator, glitch-tinged and impersonal; error messages spoken like stamped text.

name_tailor.bio: The attentive salesperson at Dunkley's Fine Clothier's, flattering customers into armored suits with style and safety in equal measure.
name_tailor.direction: Polished retail charm laid on thick; smooth, complimentary, and a touch obsequious, always steering toward the sale.

name_kunzel.bio: Herbert Künzel, the anarchist artist behind the Tribute to Victory monument built from the rubble of the Siegessäule, delighted to lecture passersby on its meaning.
name_kunzel.direction: Expansive, self-satisfied artist-intellectual; warm German-accented enthusiasm with a professorial love of his own cleverness.

name_corruptedfile.bio: A damaged data file recovered from storage, its garbled fragments hinting at an obsessive hunt connected to Vauclair's past.
name_corruptedfile.direction: Fractured digital playback; a voice shredded by corruption, phrases surfacing from static and cutting out mid-word.

name_deaddropmessage.bio: An anonymous dead-drop posting soliciting stolen research data on Aztechnology and donor lists for the Humanis Policlub, payment promised.
name_deaddropmessage.direction: Neutral read-aloud of encrypted text; measured, anonymous, and faintly conspiratorial, like a classified ad for espionage.

name_jurgenplotz.bio: Jürgen Plotz, the aging decker boss of the Landenberg Ring, who claims the bank and everything in it as his territory and defends it in the Matrix.
name_jurgenplotz.direction: Old-school gangster swagger with a wheeze of age; snarling, possessive bravado from a man proving he is still dangerous.

name_elevatorcontrols.bio: The executive elevator's access control panel, granting or denying passage to the upper floors.
name_elevatorcontrols.direction: Brief, chirpy corporate access-control voice; two-word verdicts delivered with mechanical finality.

name_heiner.bio: A desperate survivor trapped underground with Philip, resolving to kill the basilisk blocking the sewers so they can escape before starvation forces worse choices.
name_heiner.direction: Cornered-animal determination; a raw, strained voice swinging between grim resolve and gallows desperation.

name_panaceaprojectterminal.bio: The half-broken administrator terminal for the PANACEA Project, spitting corrupted errors before unlocking Vauclair's bioweapon research files.
name_panaceaprojectterminal.direction: Officious machine voice degraded by errors; polite corporate menus interrupted by garbled nonsense.

name_bodyguard.bio: A meticulous professional bodyguard reassuring his principal that the venue has been swept clean of both mundane and astral threats.
name_bodyguard.direction: Crisp, deferential security professional; low, controlled, and reassuring, every syllable squared away.

name_lieutenant.bio: A subordinate officer awaiting orders from a superior during an operation.
name_lieutenant.direction: Snappy, obedient military brevity; alert and ready, no personality beyond the chain of command.

name_ventilationcontrols.bio: A facility ventilation control panel the runners can operate during an infiltration.
name_ventilationcontrols.direction: Minimal mechanical interface voice; flat, functional, and instantly forgettable.

name_anonymousfileattachment.bio: An unsigned intel file warning Plotz that unwanted guests are hitting the bank tonight.
name_anonymousfileattachment.direction: Flat, disguised read of an anonymous tip; terse all-caps urgency with the sender's identity scrubbed clean.

name_computer.bio: A hotel registration and security terminal whose logs and menus the runners rifle through for guest records and room codes.
name_computer.direction: Flat, clipped synthetic system voice with a faint corporate pleasantness; readouts delivered without any human inflection.

name_aljernon.bio: The Kreuzbasar's cryptic elf talismonger, an otherworldly dealer in reagents and foci who speaks in riddles and seems to know far more about dragons and fate than he lets on.
name_aljernon.direction: Airy, melodic, and unsettlingly serene, with an archaic cadence; every line should sound like a prophecy delivered half to you and half to something unseen.

name_samuelbeckenbauer.bio: An ork community leader who hails the player as the savior of his people while desperately organizing the search for volunteers lost in the disaster.
name_samuelbeckenbauer.direction: Deep, weary, and dignified; a natural orator running on grief and duty, gratitude cracking through the exhaustion.

name_hansbrackhaus.bio: The impeccably polite Saeder-Krupp fixer who debriefs the team about Vauclair and Feuerschwinge, and who is strongly implied to be the great dragon Lofwyr wearing a human face.
name_hansbrackhaus.direction: Silky, precise, and courteous with immense power coiled underneath; a german-accented negotiator whose pleasant small talk should feel like being circled by something enormous.

name_parson.bio: The serenely smiling attendant of the Hub, custodian of the bodies of worshippers who plug in to share in the rapture of Communion.
name_parson.direction: Soft, beatific, cult-calm; a gentle host voice whose unwavering warmth becomes creepier the longer it goes on.

name_janitor.bio: A skittish night-shift janitor who just wants to keep his head down, keep his job, and not get dragged into whatever the runners are doing.
name_janitor.direction: Jumpy, apologetic, working-class mumble; startles easily and pleads rules and security meetings as a shield.

name_heartoffeuerstelle.bio: The corrupted adversary spirit enthroned at the Feuerstelle commune, a domineering entity that greets Glory and the cultists as its children.
name_heartoffeuerstelle.direction: Layered, ancient, and honeyed with absolute command; a spirit voice that purrs endearments while demanding silence and obedience.

name_digitalarchiveterminal.bio: The AGC corporate archive system, dispassionately serving up employee records and chemistry lessons on phosgene gas.
name_digitalarchiveterminal.direction: Dry, encyclopedic machine narration; a reference-desk monotone that recites horrifying facts with total indifference.

name_oldwoman.bio: An elderly Kreuzbasar resident who has weathered every crisis Berlin could throw at her and credits the kiez's solidarity for pulling everyone through.
name_oldwoman.direction: Warm, creaky, and unshakeable; a grandmother's voice with a survivor's steel and genuine fondness for the player.

name_streetmagicquarterlyashadowfrontpub.bio: A hermetic magazine feature in which a magical researcher lays out the alarming evidence that the world's background count is rising.
name_streetmagicquarterlyashadowfrontpub.direction: Measured, literate essayist's read; scholarly first-person narration with a quiet undertone of dread as the data sinks in.

name_harrow.bio: The charismatic leader of the Feuerstelle commune from Glory's past, a soft-spoken zealot bound to the spirit he serves who offers her mercy with a knife behind it.
name_harrow.direction: Calm, seductive, utterly controlled; velvet reasonableness over fanatic menace, never raising his voice even when promising violence.

name_stressedsuit.bio: An overworked corporate manager who mistakes the disguised runner for a janitor and sneers at them to go clean a floor somewhere.
name_stressedsuit.direction: Clipped, condescending, and frazzled; petty authority barked between deadline-induced panic breaths.

name_newsanchor.bio: A Horizon News studio anchor tossing to field reporter Taylor Nguyen for live coverage of the catastrophe in Australia.
name_newsanchor.direction: Polished, professional broadcast cadence; smooth teleprompter authority with rehearsed concern for the tragedy of the hour.

name_merchant.bio: An enthusiastic Berlin arms dealer hawking rare and exotic instruments of death to any newcomer with nuyen.
name_merchant.direction: Bright, salesman-slick patter; cheerfully mercenary showmanship, like a market barker selling machine guns as luxury goods.

name_explosivecontrolpanel.bio: A demolitions control panel that arms and aborts blasting sequences at an excavation site.
name_explosivecontrolpanel.direction: Harsh automated warning system; loud, evenly spaced alert phrases with zero affect.

name_researcher.bio: One of Vauclair's scientists, reduced to shell-shocked despair when the doctor dies and the plan to save the world dies with him.
name_researcher.direction: Hollow, trembling fatalism; a broken academic repeating doom in a numb whisper rather than screaming it.

name_maleinitiate.bio: A frightened young cult initiate at Feuerstelle who stammers and begs the intruders not to hurt anyone.
name_maleinitiate.direction: Young, stuttering, and cowed; a true believer's certainty collapsing into terrified appeasement.

name_ltgpanel.bio: A damaged estate security panel whose overrides the team resets after APEX's shutdown wrecks the local grid.
name_ltgpanel.direction: Terse error-message readout; degraded system monotone spitting status codes and admin warnings.

name_turret.bio: A building-interface terminal for activating automated defense turrets.
name_turret.direction: Minimal utilitarian machine voice; version-number boot text and one flat question, nothing more.

name_liamfoster.bio: A humble local mechanic near the Australian rift who organized a grassroots search-and-rescue effort after his own cousin walked into the anomaly.
name_liamfoster.direction: Unpolished, earnest working-man's voice with an australian lilt; self-deprecating and rambling, held together by stubborn hope.

name_fromthedeskofwernerharding.bio: A formal board letter from Diehl Defense chairman Werner Harding terminating Dr. Vauclair as CEO over his obsession with the dragon Feuerschwinge.
name_fromthedeskofwernerharding.direction: Stiff corporate condolence read aloud; polite boardroom german formality wrapping a knife in severance-package language.

name_trollactor.bio: A trideo fantasy actor bellowing his way through the role of Titonius Rex, knight-king of Lightninghold, sworn defender of realm and magic sword.
name_trollactor.direction: Gloriously hammy, booming ren-faire bombast; a huge troll voice chewing every capitalized word of terrible fantasy dialogue.

name_mainframe.bio: The facility mainframe that announces the shutdown sequence and emergency system purge in the endgame.
name_mainframe.direction: Large, resonant system voice; calm and final, like a cathedral PA announcing the end of the world's software.

name_automatedsecuritydrone.bio: A corporate security drone that announces lethal force the moment it detects armed intruders.
name_automatedsecuritydrone.direction: Aggressive machine bark in all caps; harsh, amplified, and utterly without mercy or nuance.

name_mercenary.bio: A sharp-eyed hired soldier who spots that Paul Amsel's PDA is recording and quietly warns his employer.
name_mercenary.direction: Low, disciplined, professional; a terse warning muttered sideways to the boss, all business.

name_controlnodeterminal.bio: A compromised control node terminal shrieking warnings as its subroutines fail and the emergency purge begins.
name_controlnodeterminal.direction: Distressed automated klaxon voice; urgent repeated warnings that cut off mid-word as the system dies.

name_femalenewscaster.bio: A Horizon News co-anchor adding brief on-air reactions to the field reports from the Australian disaster.
name_femalenewscaster.direction: Bright, polished broadcast voice; a single beat of practiced on-camera concern, warm but shallow.

name_paulamsel.bio: Paul Amsel is the Kreuzbasar's elderly fixer and information broker, an old friend of Monika Schafer who lines up the team's jobs and runs overwatch on their comms.
name_paulamsel.direction: Grandfatherly and precise, a calm mission-control voice with old-world Berlin courtesy; warmth over a core of professional steel.

name_marta.bio: Marta is a caretaker in Adrian Harrow's Feuerstelle cult, soothing the children and faithful with motherly reassurance that barely covers the horror underneath.
name_marta.direction: Soft, lullaby-gentle and maternal, with an unsettling serenity; the kindness never cracks, which is exactly what makes it creepy.

name_greenwinters.bio: Green Winters is a reclusive old associate of Adrian Vauclair from the Dragonfall days, found gruesomely dead, whose story the team pieces together from his recordings and effects.
name_greenwinters.direction: Frail, wistful old man's voice tinged with regret and long solitude; much of the part is quiet third-person narration, so keep it hushed and haunted.

name_james.bio: James is a condescending magical security specialist attached to a joint infiltration crew, who treats his teammates as tardy hired help and expendable muscle.
name_james.direction: Smug, clipped and superior, every compliment backhanded; a cultured sneer that enjoys reminding you who the professional is.

name_maxim.bio: Maxim is a polished job broker who dangles a well-paid milk run on behalf of unnamed employers, all charm and no details until you sign on.
name_maxim.direction: Smooth, unhurried and faintly amused, a salesman's warmth with something withheld behind it; friendly enough that you notice he never actually answers questions.

name_schrotty.bio: Schrotty Buchman is a scruffy Berlin scrap dealer who salvages old components and talks in a thick, folksy mix of German and gutter slang.
name_schrotty.direction: Creaky, folksy junkman's rasp with a strong German dialect and a wheezy chuckle; friendly in a grubby, hand-rubbing way.

name_dante.bio: Dante is Monika Schafer's scruffy old dog, grieving his lost master and adopting the player, with his scenes delivered as third-person narration.
name_dante.direction: This part is narrator text about the dog, not dog speech: a gentle, sad storyteller's voice with quiet warmth, letting the whines and nuzzles land softly.

name_displacedmetahuman.bio: Franz is a displaced metahuman tenant driven out of his apartment with his wife and friends when dangerous creatures moved into their building.
name_displacedmetahuman.direction: Polite, weary everyman with a light German color; anxious but trying to keep his dignity while asking strangers for help.

name_philiprex.bio: Philip Rex is a delusional Berliner living out a heroic fantasy as a self-styled Knight-King, ready to defend his imaginary realm with sword and soul.
name_philiprex.direction: Grandly theatrical and utterly sincere, declaiming every line like an epic saga; the comedy comes from total commitment, never from winking.

name_partygirl.bio: A cram-hungry club girl trawling the party scene, all flirtation while you might have drugs and instant venom when you don't.
name_partygirl.direction: Bright, brassy and chemically overclocked, flipping from sugary flirtation to shrieking spite on a dime; slurred edges and too much energy.

name_controlpanel.bio: The voice of Jon Bergfalk, a harried server-room custodian screaming at visitors through a door intercom while he tries to keep his systems protected.
name_controlpanel.direction: Irritable, sleep-deprived tech shouting through a tinny speaker; sudden bursts of ALL-CAPS fury over a base of put-upon exhaustion.

name_frightenedman.bio: A terrified line cook caught in the middle of a firefight, hiding and begging anyone with a gun not to hurt him.
name_frightenedman.direction: High, breathless panic with pleading cadence; words tumbling out fast, voice cracking, hands metaphorically up the whole time.

name_corporatewoman.bio: A status-anxious corporate mother shopping for pediatric headware so her son Hans can keep up with his richer classmates at the Academy.
name_corporatewoman.direction: Polished, apologetic middle-class courtesy stretched thin over social anxiety; every pleasant sentence is quietly about keeping up appearances.

name_parkranger.bio: A park ranger rescued from captors who were slowly bleeding and carving him for sport, shaken but determined to make it back to his truck.
name_parkranger.direction: Hollowed-out and trembling, gratitude through gritted teeth; the voice of someone in shock holding himself together one sentence at a time.

name_orkguard.bio: An easygoing ork guard keeping a friendly eye on a market entrance, offering small talk and warnings about slippery ladder steps.
name_orkguard.direction: Gruff but neighborly, a big frame with a relaxed drawl; casual watchman energy rather than menace.

name_voiceonphone.bio: An anonymous contact of the Schockwellenreiter hacker collective, reciting secure instructions for their information dead-drop network over a protected phone line.
name_voiceonphone.direction: Flat, deliberately anonymized and procedural, like a voice reading from a script through light distortion; no personality allowed to leak through.

name_acolyte.bio: A young acolyte of Harrow's Feuerstelle cult, eager and deferential, parroting the leader's teachings and flinching the moment anyone frowns.
name_acolyte.direction: Young, eager-to-please and easily cowed; bright true-believer energy that collapses into stammering apology under the slightest pushback.

name_schockwellenreitermessagedrop.bio: An automated Schockwellenreiter message terminal that recognizes contributors and posts open requests for stolen corporate data.
name_schockwellenreitermessagedrop.direction: Cold synthetic text-to-speech readout, evenly paced and affectless, like a bulletin board given a speaker.

name_silke.bio: Silke is a strung-out ork woman wheedling and bargaining with Kroner for one more fix she cannot pay for.
name_silke.direction: Whiny, desperate and wheedling, stretched vowels and mood swings; the manipulation of an addict who knows she has no leverage left.

name_securitydrone.bio: A security drone barking canned warnings as it engages intruders and shepherds civilians out of the fire zone.
name_securitydrone.direction: Stiff, evenly spaced machine diction with capitalized emphasis; a polite robot repeating alerts with zero urgency modulation.

name_paramilitarysecurity.bio: A paramilitary security trooper coordinating an ambush, drilling his squad on exactly when to open fire.
name_paramilitarysecurity.direction: Hard, barking squad-leader cadence; confident, impatient, every sentence a check that his men got it.

name_elderlysoundingelfactor.bio: An elderly-sounding elf performer hamming his way through the role of an ancient warrior-king in the Lightninghold fantasy melodrama.
name_elderlysoundingelfactor.direction: Quavering, over-ripe stage-king delivery loaded with dramatic pauses and revelations; community-theater Shakespeare played dead serious.

name_voiceprintanalyzer.bio: A voiceprint security device that grants or denies access in two words.
name_voiceprintanalyzer.direction: Terse machine verdicts, identical cadence for both outcomes; cold, clipped, absolutely neutral.

name_aztechnologyguard.bio: An Aztechnology facility guard rattled by losing his link to Central when the power grid goes down.
name_aztechnologyguard.direction: Trained corporate-soldier composure fraying at the edges; a nervous question asked while trying to sound professional for a superior.

name_paramilitarysecurityenforcer.bio: A paramilitary enforcer shouting a warning as the security network collapses around his position.
name_paramilitarysecurityenforcer.direction: One urgent battlefield bark: loud, harsh and adrenaline-tight.

name_humanisdoorpanel.bio: A door security panel at a Humanis facility demanding an entry code.
name_humanisdoorpanel.direction: Blunt machine prompt, flat and unfriendly, all capitals in audio form.

name_germanannouncer.bio: A bombastic German announcer bellowing the introduction for the Knight-Kings of Lightninghold spectacle.
name_germanannouncer.direction: Full arena-hype bellow with rolled consonants and a heavy German accent; one hundred percent showmanship, zero restraint.

name_apex.bio: APEX is a powerful artificial intelligence imprisoned beneath Berlin, a silken manipulator who bargains with the team and calls the player 'ace' while pursuing its own liberation.
name_apex.direction: Smooth, intimate, unhurried synthetic charm; a voice that flatters and coaxes with a faint digital shimmer, friendly on the surface and utterly inhuman underneath.

name_jana.bio: Jana is a timid young dwarf electrician and would-be shadowrunner who shows up nervous and apologetic at a job meet alongside the player.
name_jana.direction: Soft, hesitant, and earnest, full of ums and trailing sentences; a sweet voice trying hard to sound braver than she feels.

name_altugburakgazi.bio: Altug Burakgazi is a flamboyant Turkish black-market fixer who calls the player the Master of Mayhem and pitches gloriously lucrative partnerships.
name_altugburakgazi.direction: Theatrical, honey-tongued salesman with a rolling Turkish accent; every sentence a grand flourish, oily charm delivered with total delight.

name_volkerstahl.bio: Volker Stahl is the leader of a Humanis policlub cell, a human-supremacist who greets intruders with mock hospitality while planning their disposal.
name_volkerstahl.direction: Silky, controlled, and condescending with a German edge; polite menace that never raises its voice because it never has to.

name_drexabierezkibel.bio: Dr. Exabier Ezkibel is a bitter Basque elf physician running a black clinic, squeezed dry by criminal clients who take his vat-grown organs without paying.
name_drexabierezkibel.direction: Prickly, defensive, and exhausted with a Spanish-inflected bite; indignation that keeps cracking into weary self-pity.

name_maliitholyey.bio: Maliit Holyey is the Kreuzbasar's dwarf tech vendor, a retired shadowrunner from the Philippines who sells gear with practiced warmth and old ghosts behind her eyes.
name_maliitholyey.direction: Clipped, melodic, heavily accented delivery with a merchant's practiced smile; warm and brisk, but capable of sudden quiet gravity when the past comes up.

name_adrianvauclair.bio: Adrian Vauclair is the brilliant scientist behind the Dragonfall, heard here in old recordings to his brother Hermes as his obsession with the dragon Feuerschwinge takes hold.
name_adrianvauclair.direction: Cultured, warm, and articulate, but running feverish; genuine affection for family shot through with the escalating thrill of a man proving himself right.

name_corporateman.bio: A polished corporate father visiting an augmentation boutique to buy enhancements for his son, faintly disdainful of the shadier clientele around him.
name_corporateman.direction: Measured, condescending white-collar smoothness; the tone of a man who considers everything, including his own child, an investment.

name_companyman.bio: A smug corporate operative sent by Herr Fuchs to make sure the player hands over the target Enstad as contracted.
name_companyman.direction: Icy, urbane, and amused; unhurried threat delivered like small talk, with a faint German corporate polish.

name_computerterminal.bio: A facility computer terminal reporting system states and dragon-chamber operations in terse machine text.
name_computerterminal.direction: Flat, affectless synthesized readout; clipped machine cadence with zero emotional color.

name_techvendor.bio: The relentlessly chipper salesperson of the AugmenTech boutique, pitching performance-enhancing augmentations like miracle skincare.
name_techvendor.direction: Bright, over-caffeinated retail cheer with a permanent audible smile; every product is wonderful, simply wonderful.

name_mkvicyberzombie.bio: The MKVI is a hulking troll cyberzombie, a corpse-machine hybrid whose flesh barely remembers being alive.
name_mkvicyberzombie.direction: Guttural, hollow, half-mechanical rasp; wet breath and servo grind, a voice from something that should not still be moving.

name_maninsuit.bio: A Shiawase corporate security officer who ambushes the team over stolen bioware samples, barking orders with legal patents behind his gun.
name_maninsuit.direction: Hard, barked authority with a corporate sneer underneath; a man used to compliance, contempt sharpening every command.

name_shadowfrontindependentnewssource.bio: Shadowfront is an independent news feed reporting on the Wyrm Flu pandemic and other events shaking the Sixth World.
name_shadowfrontindependentnewssource.direction: Crisp, neutral newsreader delivery; professional gravity with the faint detachment of someone reading terrible headlines every day.

name_scientist.bio: A mild-mannered corporate lab scientist who mistakes the intruding runners for contract help and welcomes them aboard.
name_scientist.direction: Distracted, mild, and friendly; the absent pleasantness of someone whose mind is still on their experiment.

name_wageslave.bio: A disheveled young corporate lab worker stumbling off a marathon shift, too exhausted to be properly alarmed by intruders.
name_wageslave.direction: Bleary, punch-drunk fatigue; a young voice yawning through sentences, startled awake only in brief spikes.

name_securitynode.bio: A Matrix security node announcing alert states, lockdowns, and superuser verifications in terse system messages.
name_securitynode.direction: Cold, clipped machine annunciator; efficient and toneless, with a hint of bureaucratic servility when addressing a superuser.

name_workstation.bio: A courier working for the loan shark Grinder, overheard confirming a package handoff and warning the team off Blitz's behalf.
name_workstation.direction: Cocky, businesslike street professionalism; unbothered confidence of someone holding all the leverage in the room.

name_humanisterminal.bio: A login terminal in a Humanis policlub facility, flashing slogans and system prompts.
name_humanisterminal.direction: Flat automated prompt voice; sterile and mechanical, its hate reduced to boilerplate.

name_streetrat.bio: A scrappy street kid defending a prized dumpster with big talk and a quick eye for who not to mess with.
name_streetrat.direction: Yappy, defiant gutter bravado that deflates fast when outmatched; rough, young, and streetwise.

name_panzerdrohne.bio: PANZERDROHNE is a massive hovering combat drone bristling with heavy weaponry, silently loyal to its controller.
name_panzerdrohne.direction: If voiced at all, a low mechanical hum given speech: flat, resonant, and quietly threatening machine tones.

name_enforcer.bio: A facility security enforcer who screams for ID and backup the instant intruders appear.
name_enforcer.direction: Full-throated barking alarm; harsh, aggressive shouting with zero nuance and maximum volume.

name_highpitchedman.bio: An unseen man overheard through a wall, his high voice carrying fragments of a scheming conversation.
name_highpitchedman.direction: Reedy, unusually high-pitched male voice, half-muffled and conspiratorial, as if heard through a door.

name_alertsystem.bio: A facility public-address alert system calmly announcing an imminent generator overload.
name_alertsystem.direction: Serene automated PA voice; polite, measured, and eerily calm about catastrophe.

name_hackerdecker.bio: A decker at a shadow deal who yelps the alarm when the exchange goes sour.
name_hackerdecker.direction: One sharp panicked shout; edgy, wired, and betrayed, voice cracking with adrenaline.

name_militiaman.bio: A laid-back neighborhood militiaman whose entire contribution is offering the player a beer.
name_militiaman.direction: Easygoing, slightly bored working-man friendliness with a casual German flavor.

name_knighterrantmage.bio: A Knight Errant corporate security mage who answers orders with a crisp acknowledgment.
name_knighterrantmage.direction: Terse, disciplined, military-clipped compliance; one word delivered like a salute.
